findings: Verified as an adult male Eastern Towhee (Pipilo erythrophthalmus): sooty-black hood and upperparts, rufous flanks, clean white belly, stout triangular bill, reddish-brown eye, pinkish legs, white wing markings, and long rounded tail with white outer corners agree with the field-reference photos and sources.
All visible plate text is supported: the stated length, wingspan, and mass match the verified range; habitat, ground-foraging behavior, and listed field marks are consistent with source descriptions.
Anatomy is coherent: exactly one head, one beak, two legs, one tail, and two wings (one folded wing visible and the far wing naturally occluded).
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: Pipilo erythrophthalmus (eastern towhee) | Animal Diversity Web
